name: Association for Computing Machinery (ACM)
blurb: The CS department's home club: hackathons, interview prep nights, and the upperclassmen who already interned where you want to. Cheapest career insurance in the major.

name: IEEE Student Branch
blurb: Hardware-side counterpart to ACM — solder nights, industry talks, and the national IEEE resume badge that EE recruiters actually recognize.

name: Razorbotz Robotics
blurb: Build competition robots across EE/ME/CS lines. A robot that survived a NASA competition is a senior-design story you get to tell as a sophomore.

name: AI & Data Science Groups
blurb: Student AI/ML and data-science orgs run project teams and Kaggle-style competitions — the portfolio pieces data recruiters ask about. Names shift year to year; search HogSync for the active one.

name: Society of Women Engineers (SWE)
blurb: One of the strongest employer pipelines in the College of Engineering — the SWE conference is functionally a career fair with a membership card.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME)
blurb: Design-build competitions and plant tours; the chapter network is how ME students find the internships that never hit job boards.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE)
blurb: Concrete canoe and steel bridge teams are the civil resume's proof-of-work — and Garver/Olsson engineers judge the competitions.

name: Institute of Industrial & Systems Engineers (IISE)
blurb: Industrial engineering's professional org — lean/six-sigma workshops and direct lines to the operations teams at NWA's logistics giants.

name: Engineers Without Borders (EWB)
blurb: Real infrastructure projects for real communities — the rare club that gives you both engineering scope and a service story in one line.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers (AIChE)
blurb: ChemE car competitions and industry nights; the chapter is where process-engineering internships circulate first.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society (BMES)
blurb: The BMEG department's hub for lab openings, med-tech industry talks, and grad-school panels from students who just got in.

name: Enactus
blurb: Social-entrepreneurship project teams with a national competition circuit — Walton's flagship for students who want to build things, not just study them.

name: Finance & Investment Clubs (Walton)
blurb: Stock pitches, portfolio management, and alumni panels from banking and PE. If you want Wall Street from Arkansas, your pitch-competition record is your transcript. Search HogSync for the active finance orgs.

name: Supply Chain Student Orgs (Walton)
blurb: Walton's SCM orgs run site visits inside Walmart and J.B. Hunt operations and host the recruiters themselves. Membership is a soft first-round interview.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
blurb: The accounting/finance honor org — Big Four firms recruit through its meetings, and the GPA bar itself signals discipline.

name: American Marketing Association (AMA)
blurb: Client projects and case competitions that produce actual campaign portfolios — the thing marketing hiring managers ask for and classes rarely provide.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta (Pre-Health)
blurb: The pre-health society: MCAT resources, physician shadowing connections, and volunteering pipelines — the logistics layer of a med-school application.

name: Psi Chi & Psychology Club
blurb: Psych grad programs expect research hours; these orgs are where lab RA openings get announced first. Pair membership with volunteering in a lab from the directory.

name: Pre-Law Society
blurb: LSAT prep, law-school admissions panels, and mock trial adjacency — plus the writing-heavy campus jobs that make a law application read well.

name: Society of Physics Students
blurb: Small department, tight club: research openings, REU application help, and professors at pizza nights. The path into quantum-materials labs starts here.

name: American Chemical Society Student Chapter
blurb: Chemistry's professional org on campus — demo outreach, research mixers, and the membership line every chemistry grad application carries.

name: Poultry Science Club
blurb: In the poultry capital of the world, this club is a professional network: Tyson, Simmons and George's sponsor events and hire from the judging teams by name.

name: Block & Bridle
blurb: Animal science's marquee club — livestock shows, industry trips, and the hands-on record ag employers screen for.

name: Food Science Club
blurb: Product-development competitions judged by the industry next door. College Bowl teams here have national wins — and R&D internships follow.

name: Student Nurses' Association
blurb: Clinical volunteering coordination, NCLEX prep culture, and hospital connections — the professional on-ramp for nursing students.

name: Student Media — The Arkansas Traveler, UATV, KXUA
blurb: The student paper, TV station and radio station are working newsrooms with real audiences — published clips and produced segments are the portfolio that gets media jobs.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS)
blurb: Fay Jones students' professional org — firm crawls, portfolio reviews, and the mentorship that demystifies licensure.

name: Public Relations Student Society (PRSSA)
blurb: Client work for real organizations plus the national PRSSA network — communications hiring runs on exactly these portfolios and references.
